behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response." Slot B: model-output. Slot C: If lowered to Integration, a stubbed LLM can always return a perfectly grounded draft, missing real failures where the model outputs generic text despite correct prompt wiring. Rejection-gate: Workflow was considered, but the behavior text only targets one model judgment (draft grounding), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence. Final: Agentic because the decisive boundary is model-output (Slot B), and Integration would hide the miss in Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real LLM with a real-looking thread containing distinct facts (names, dates, commitments). Assert the draft correctly cites multiple thread-specific points and fails when output is generic or ignores key thread details.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base." Slot B: model-output. Slot C: If lowered to Integration, deterministic contradiction fixtures can pass while the real checker misses nuanced contradictions or produces inconsistent contradiction judgments. Rejection-gate: Workflow was considered, but this behavior isolates one decision boundary (contradiction detection), not a multi-step agentic chain. Final: Agentic because correctness depends on real contradiction judgment quality (Slot B), and lower-level stubs hide the miss in Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real model-backed consistency checker on drafts containing explicit and subtle KB contradictions. Verify contradictory claims are flagged and non-contradictory claims are not over-flagged across repeated runs/tolerances.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts" when KB is irrelevant. Slot B: model-output. Slot C: If lowered to Integration, a mocked generator can always include the disclaimer, missing real failures where the model hallucinates confident facts when retrieval is empty. Rejection-gate: Workflow was considered, but behavior text evaluates one generation decision under a no-evidence condition, not a sequence of multiple agentic decisions. Final: Agentic because the key failure is in model behavior under uncertainty (Slot B), and lower tests miss fabrication risk (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: Execute with real retrieval returning no relevant KB items and a real LLM draft step. Assert output contains the required uncertainty disclaimer and does not introduce unsupported factual claims.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically." Slot B: handoff (state/orchestration boundary between approval state and send module). Slot C: If lowered to Unit, you can miss failures where UI/service state is not propagated and sender is invoked before approval. Integration-brake: (a) real OS/network/subprocess required? No, sender can be an in-process fake. (b) depends on real AI/ML output? No. (c) multiple agentic steps? No. (d) LLM-governed handoff correctness? No, this is deterministic approval gating. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but the phrase "blocks sending until user explicitly approves" is a local orchestration rule and does not require real external transport behavior to expose the core failure. Final: Integration due to module handoff (Slot B) and the cross-module miss in Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Wire approval controller, draft state store, and send service in-process. Attempt send before approval and assert send service is never called; then approve and assert exactly one send call occurs.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end." Slot B: multi-step agentic (generation plus model-dependent fact-check behavior chained with handoffs). Slot C: If lowered to Agentic, single-step checks can pass while failures in cross-step state carryover/order (thread context lost before fact-check or approval payload mismatch) remain undetected. Final: Workflow because correctness depends on multiple sequenced agentic stages and their handoffs (Slot B), with concrete cross-step misses at the lower level (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: Run the full pipeline on a real thread artifact through all stages in one execution: ingest thread, generate via real LLM, run factual check, and surface approval artifact. Assert stage outputs are correctly handed off and the final presented draft reflects both grounding and fact-check outcomes.
